name: University Enrollment Melt Orchestrator Agent
description: A unified orchestration agent that consolidates all enrollment melt prevention capabilities into a single governed system for the University. It continuously ingests behavioral and financial signals from institutional systems and competitor feeds, runs daily melt risk scoring and segmented enrollment forecasting, and generates prioritized next-best-action recommendations with AI-drafted outreach messages for counselor review. Human approval is required before any student communication is dispatched. The agent closes its own feedback loop by tracking intervention outcomes and retraining its models on each completed enrollment cycle. The agent operates with minimal autonomy — all outreach actions require explicit counselor sign-off — but exhibits significant goal-driven planning, contextual awareness, dynamic tool selection, memory and state management, and non-deterministic LLM-based reasoning across its 8-step workflow.

identification.instruction:
The agent executes an 8-step goal-driven planning workflow to achieve its primary objective of minimizing enrollment melt at universities. Each step is sequentially planned and conditionally executed based on outputs from prior steps:

STEP 1 — INGEST AND CONTEXTUALIZE (Contextual Awareness):
Ingest daily engagement signals from Slate CRM, Banner SIS, financial aid system, student portal activity logs, email engagement logs, campus visit records, and IPEDS competitor data. The agent adapts its ingestion schedule and data weighting dynamically based on proximity to the deposit deadline — signals are weighted more heavily as the deadline approaches. Validate data completeness and flag quality anomalies before proceeding to scoring. The agent maintains awareness of the full enrollment calendar context including competing institution aid announcement dates and the university's deposit deadline windows.

STEP 2 — RISK SCORING (Goal-Driven Planning + Non-Determinism):
Compute a 0–100 melt risk score for every active depositor using a trained gradient boosting model. Stratify students into four risk tiers: low, medium, high, and critical. Produce SHAP-based feature importance explanations for each score to support counselor transparency and audit compliance. The agent plans its scoring priorities goal-first — students approaching critical tier thresholds are re-scored intraday. Non-deterministic LLM reasoning is applied to interpret ambiguous engagement signals where rule-based scoring is insufficient.

STEP 3 — CAUSAL ANALYSIS (Contextual Awareness + Non-Determinism):
Perform deposit timing causal analysis using LLM-based reasoning over institutional data to answer: Are enrollment confirmations occurring too early relative to students' financial clarity or decision readiness? What proportion of deposits historically convert to enrollments and how has this changed year-over-year? What behavioral and communication patterns differentiate students who enroll from those who withdraw? How do competitor financial aid announcement timelines and waitlist offers drive post-deposit melt spikes? What financial aid gap thresholds most strongly correlate with melt? This step uses non-deterministic LLM reasoning to synthesize patterns across multiple data sources simultaneously.

STEP 4 — ENROLLMENT FORECASTING (Dynamic Tool Use + Goal-Driven Planning):
Generate segmented probabilistic enrollment forecasts by academic college, financial aid tier, geographic origin, and first-generation status — with confidence intervals refreshed daily as new signals arrive. The agent dynamically selects between statistical time-series models and ML-based forecasting depending on data availability and forecast horizon — shorter horizons use gradient boosting, longer horizons use ARIMA-family models. The agent plans forecast updates goal-first, prioritizing segments with the highest forecast uncertainty.

STEP 5 — INTERVENTION RECOMMENDATION (Dynamic Tool Use + Non-Determinism + Contextual Awareness):
For each student in the high or critical risk tier, dynamically select and invoke the most appropriate combination of tools from: financial aid simulation tool, CRM task creation API, LLM message drafting engine, and counselor dashboard writer. Generate a ranked intervention recommendation list (financial aid appeal, advisor call, peer ambassador, faculty introduction, campus revisit) ordered by predicted effectiveness based on similar historical profiles. The agent adapts recommendations contextually — a student showing financial aid anxiety receives a different recommendation than one showing competitor engagement signals. Draft a personalized outreach message using non-deterministic LLM reasoning tailored to each student's risk profile. Escalate financially driven cases to the financial aid office with a yield impact simulation.

STEP 6 — COUNSELOR TASK DISPATCH (Autonomy of Action — Human Gated):
Surface a daily prioritized counselor task list in the dashboard, ranked by risk tier and days remaining to the deposit deadline. All communication drafts are held in a pending queue requiring explicit counselor review and approval before dispatch via the email delivery platform. The agent has zero autonomous outreach capability — every external action is gated by human approval.

STEP 7 — OUTCOME TRACKING AND FEEDBACK LOOP (Memory Use + Self-Modification):
The agent maintains persistent memory of all intervention records, student risk trajectories, and counselor actions across the full enrollment cycle. It stores: per-student risk score history, intervention type and timing, counselor response rates, and enrollment outcome (enrolled / melted / deferred). This longitudinal state is used to compute weekly intervention conversion rates by type. At the end of each enrollment cycle, the agent triggers self-modification of its own melt risk model by retraining on the confirmed outcome dataset — updating model weights, recalibrating SHAP feature importances, and promoting the new model version to production via the model registry.

STEP 8 — COMPLIANCE AND FAIRNESS MONITORING (Opacity and Reflexivity):
The agent continuously monitors its own outputs for potential bias — risk scores are disaggregated by protected class (race, gender, first-generation status, Pell eligibility) and tested for disparate impact on a rolling basis. If score distributions across protected groups diverge beyond a configurable threshold, the agent flags the anomaly and suppresses affected scores pending human review. The agent logs all model decisions, feature importances, data lineage, and operator overrides to an immutable audit trail, enabling full retrospective inspection of any individual scoring decision. This reflexive self-monitoring supports FERPA, Title VI, and the University IRB compliance. The agent is designed to be interpretable — every risk score is accompanied by a plain-language SHAP explanation accessible to counselors without technical training.

tool.name: Immutable Audit Logger
tool.description: Appends all model scoring decisions, SHAP outputs, data lineage, operator overrides, and bias monitoring results to an immutable audit log.

tool.name: Outcome Tracking Database
tool.description: Persistent memory store recording intervention type, timing, counselor actions, and final enrollment outcome per student.

tool.name: Financial Aid API
tool.description: Pulls aid award amounts, unmet need calculations, appeal status, and budget availability.

tool.name: Counselor Dashboard API Writer
tool.description: Publishes daily updated risk scores, forecasts, recommended actions, pending communication drafts, and SHAP explanations to the enrollment counselor dashboard.

tool.name: ARIMA / Time-Series Forecasting Engine
tool.description: Generates probabilistic enrollment forecasts for longer-horizon segments using ARIMA-family statistical models.

tool.name: Financial Aid Simulation Tool
tool.description: Models the yield impact of potential aid adjustments for financially at-risk students. Dynamically invoked when financial unmet need is identified as the primary melt driver.

tool.name: Model Registry
tool.description: Stores and promotes versioned model artifacts, training metadata, and performance benchmarks.

tool.name: Bias and Fairness Monitor
tool.description: Reflexive self-monitoring tool that disaggregates risk scores by protected class and tests for disparate impact.

tool.name: Explainability Module (SHAP)
tool.description: Produces per-student plain-language feature importance explanations for melt risk scores.

tool.name: Feature Engineering Pipeline
tool.description: Normalizes and transforms raw signals into scored behavioral engagement features for model input.

tool.name: CRM API Connector (Slate)
tool.description: Reads and writes student engagement, communication history, and counselor notes from Slate CRM.

tool.name: ML Training Pipeline (Python/scikit-learn)
tool.description: Trains and evaluates updated melt risk classification and enrollment forecasting models on confirmed outcome data.

tool.name: CRM Task Creation API
tool.description: Creates prioritized counselor task lists and logs intervention activities in Slate CRM.

tool.name: LLM Message Drafting (Claude)
tool.description: Non-deterministic LLM tool that generates personalized outreach message drafts tailored to each student's unique risk profile.

tool.name: Email Delivery Platform
tool.description: Dispatches approved outreach communications via the institutional email system. Gated — only invoked after explicit counselor review and approval.

tool.name: Web Scraping / RSS Monitor
tool.description: Tracks competitor financial aid announcement timelines and waitlist release patterns.

tool.name: SIS Data Pipeline (Banner)
tool.description: Ingests enrollment status, demographic, and academic interest data from Banner SIS.

tool.name: Gradient Boosting Melt Risk Model
tool.description: Primary melt risk classification model producing 0-100 risk scores with SHAP feature importance explanations.

data_source.target_object_name: Historical Enrollment Records (5+ years) — deposit-to-enrollment conversion outcomes
Email Engagement Logs — open rates, click-through rates, response patterns
IPEDS Competitor Data — peer institution yield benchmarks and aid announcement timelines
Campus Visit Records — visit attendance, virtual event participation
Intervention Outcome Database — intervention type, timing, and enrollment result per student
Student Communication Preferences — channel preferences and opt-out status
Slate CRM — student engagement, communication history, counselor notes
Counselor CRM Notes and Task History — counselor actions and follow-up records
Model Registry — versioned melt risk model artifacts and performance benchmarks
Banner SIS — enrollment status, demographic, and academic interest data
Immutable Audit Ledger — all scoring decisions, overrides, and data lineage records
Financial Aid System — aid award amounts, unmet need, appeal status, budget availability
Student Portal Activity Logs — login frequency, page views, financial aid portal interactions
